2606.24974.title: Quantifying Explainable AI-introduced signal noise on ECG data with Spectral Entropy
2606.24974.paper_summary: Explainability techniques are used to assess the output of various deep learning models. This is especially true in healthcare, where models need to be trusted and decisions justified. Explainability (XAI) tools use heuristics which often add signal noise to the explanation "core". It is not always obvious what is signal from the model and what is noise from the XAI. We propose the use of spectral entropy as a measure of noise in XAI output. We demonstrate its usefulness in the context of classifying arrhythmias in an ECG dataset with different post hoc explainability techniques.
2606.24974.paper_summary_zh: 解釋性技術用於評估各種深度學習模型的輸出。這在醫療保健領域尤為重要，因為模型需要被信任，並且決策需要有理據。解釋性（XAI）工具使用的啟發式方法常常會為解釋的“核心”添加信號噪聲。從模型中分辨出信號和來自XAI的噪聲並不總是明顯。我們提出使用光譜熵作為XAI輸出中噪聲的衡量標準。我們展示了它在使用不同的事後解釋技術對ECG數據集中的心律不整進行分類時的實用性。
2606.24974.author: David A. Kelly et.al.
